publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 48-49. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: My Dearest. I cannot describe how pleased I was to see ou dear letter this morning, which I had quietly resigned myself to as a no-post day. I almost thought at first that it was the ghost of a letter.-- I conclude Postsmouth is the solution of the mystery.

Had I known this, I should have written to you on Saturday.-- I am just going down to the pier-- at least as soon as it is dark, to look through a telescope at the stars. I did not tell you of the treat I had a day or two ago in going to hear the Enniskillen regiment band-- a very good one-- which plays on the pier occasionally.--
I met Emily today walking. I was in my

chair. I stopped & had a parley with her. I thought she seemed in good humour with me. The Hen has asked her to drink tea with her tomorrow or next day.-- The Hen has been sailing today, & also driving to Rotting Dean in the phaeton with Hester.
My Dearest, I long to see ou. I am very much in want of my dear Master.--
